properties.remark: LOCAL MEDIA REPORTS NC HIGHWAY PATROL SAYS 2 LANES OF WESTBOUND I-40 HAS BEEN CLOSED DUE TO A DEBRIS FLOW. IT IS EXPECTED TO BE CLEARED QUICKLY THIS EVENING.
A MUDSLIDE WAS REPORTED SATURDAY MORNING ON U.S. HIGHWAY 19 NEAR HEWITTS ROAD. THE ROAD WAS IMPASSABLE FOLLOWING THE SLIDE BUT ONE LANE OF TRAVEL WAS OPENED LATER IN THE DAY.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING OF EAST FORK BARKERS CREEK, WASHING OUT SEVERAL CULVERTS ALONG ROCKDALE RD. INCLUDING AT ADDRESSES 400, 901, AND 1001.

JACKSON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED STREAM FLOODING AT THE CONFLUENCE OF EAST FORK BARKERS CREEK AND WEST FORK BARKERS CREEK AND THE INTERSECTION OF ROCKDALE RD AND BARKERS CREEK RD, FORCING ROAD CLOSURE. FLOODWATERS PRODUCED UP TO 12 INUNDATION AT THE ADJACENT ROCKDALE BAPTIST CHURCH.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED BARKERS CREEK WASHED OUT THE DRIVEWAY FOR THE ROCKDALE BAPTIST CHURCH FELLOWSHIP HALL AND ADJACENT CEMETARY.

TREES DOWN ON HIGHWAY 18 IN GAMEWELL.

EMERGENCY MNGR REPORTED DICKS CREEK FLOODED DICKS CREEK RD, TRAPPING A DEPUTY SHERIFF WHO REQUIRED RESCUE. THE FLOODWATERS DAMAGED THE ROAD SURFACE IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS ALONG DICKS CREEK RD AND CAUSED SEVERAL PARTIAL WASHOUTS.

THE STREAMGAUGE ON THE FRENCH BROAD RIVER ABOVE US 178 AT ROSMAN EXCEEDED THE MINOR FLOOD STAGE OF 9.0 FEET AT 2:45AM. THE FRENCH BROAD RIVER CRESTED AT 12.58 FEET, OR 0.42 FEET BELOW MODERATE FLOOD STAGE, AT 5:00AM. AT THIS LEVEL FLOODING OF CHAMPION PARK AND SECTIONS OF US 178, DEPOT ST, AND MAIN ST OCCURS WITH FLOODWATERS APPROACHING SEVERAL HOMES AND BUSINESSES.
TREES DOWN ON REVISTA DR AND CENTRAL DRIVE NEAR CENTRAL.

NWS STORM SURVEY REPORTED TORNADO ON 34.5155 N 83.3671 W OR 2 S BOYDVILLE IN STEPHENS COUNTY GA.

TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS FRANKLIN COUNTY. HARDEST HIT AREA WAS THE GUMLOG COMMUNITY. SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE INCLUDING TREES AND LIMBS ON STRUCTURES. DAMAGE INCLUDES WITHIN CARNESVILLE. SOME RESIDENTS STILL WITHOUT POWER THE DAY AFTER THE STORM.

REPORT OF TREE DOWN ON LEBANON RD NEAR PENDLETON SQUARE BLOCKING ROADWAY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MACON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON GIBSON COVE BRANCH AT OLD MURPHY RD. ONE TO TWO FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING OVER OLD MURPHY RD AND UP TO 2 FEET OF INUNDATION OCCURRED INSIDE THE DOLLAR GENERAL ADJACENT TO THE CREEK. A NEARBY COMMERCIAL FACILITY SAW 2-3 FEET OF INUNDATION WITH FLOOD DAMAGE TO VEHICLES AND STORAGE BUILDINGS. FLOODING LESS THAN 1 FOOT DEEP OF OTHER NEARBY COMMERCIAL PARKING LOTS ALSO OCCURRED. SEVERAL AREAS OF FLOODING IN AND AROUND FRANKLIN WERE REPORTED AROUND THE SAME TIME, BUT THIS LOCATION SAW THE MOST SIGNIFICANT IMPACTS. APPROXIMATELY 4-6 OF RAIN FELL IN ABOUT 7-8 HOURS ACROSS THE CARTOOGECHAYE CREEK WATERSHED. LAT:35.166960, LON:-83.433916
FLOODING IMPACTED 15 OR MORE HOUSES NEAR THE E PIGEON RIVER ALONG AND NEAR HIGHWAY 276 BELOW THE CRUSO FIRE DEPARTMENT. ALSO HAD SEVERAL CARS WASHED AWAY.

MACON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A MODIFIED SLOPE FAILURE ON MOREWOOD CIR. THE FAILURE DAMAGED A HIGH-PRESSURE WATER LINE AND THREATENED AN ADJACENT HOME BUT HAS NOT YET CAUSED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. LAT 35.041540 LON -83.208640

MACON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED SEVERAL DEBRIS FLOWS IMPACTING SLOPES ADJACENT TO PARKER FARM RD, MAXWELL HOME RD, TURKEY PEN RD, AND ENCHANTED MOUNTAIN RD. THE FIRST FLOW OCCURRED AT 500AM EDT. THE NORTH CAROLINA GEOLOGICAL SURVEY SURVEYED AT LEAST 5 DISTINCT DEBRIS FLOWS ALONG THE UNMODIFIED NORTHWEST-FACING AND SOUTHEAST-FACING SLOPES OF DOBSON MOUNTAIN. THE NORTHWEST-FACING DEBRIS FLOW...LOCATED NEAR LAT 35.143097 LON -83.460834...SCOURED OUT A PATH 4000 FEET LONG BY 250-350 FEET WIDE...IMPACTING A 16.8 ACRE AREA WITH A TOTAL VOLUME OF AT LEAST 12000 CUBIC YARDS. A COMMERCIAL FACILITY WAS DAMAGED AT 595 MAXWELL HOME RD AND DAMAGE TO RESIDENTIAL BUILDINGS OCCURRED AT 504 TURKEY PEN RD. SIGNIFICANT ROAD DAMAGE OCCURRED AT TURKEY PEN RD AND ENCHANTED MOUNTAIN RD.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND THE PATH OF AN EF2 TORNADO ACROSS THE NORTH SIDE OF GREENVILLE, BEGINNING JUST EAST OF CHERRYDALE POINT AND MOVING EAST/SOUTHEAST ACROSS PLEASANTBURG DR, RUTHERFORD RD, AND WADE HAMPTON BLVD, CROSSING THROUGH A RESIDENTIAL AREA BETWEEN WADE HAMPTON BLVD AND E NORTH ST BEFORE LIFTING. ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF A HOTEL AND RESTAURANT ON RUTHERFORD RD, AND SEVERAL OTHER BUSINESSES RECEIVED MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE ALONG WADE HAMPTON BLVD. OTHERWISE, NUMEROUS HARDWOODS WERE SNAPPED AND SOME PARTIALLY DEBARKED. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES.

ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF A HOTEL AND RESTAURANT ON RUTHERFORD RD, AND SEVERAL OTHER BUSINESSES RECEIVED MINOR TO MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE ALONG WADE HAMPTON BLVD. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAMS FROM GREENVILLE-SPARTANBURG AND COLUMBIA SC HAVE CONFIRMED A TORNADO BEGAN IN SOUTHEASTERN CHESTER COUNTY AND DISSIPATED IN SOUTHWESTERN LANCASTER COUNTY. THE TORNADO PRODUCED WINDS UP TO 115 MPH, WHICH IS AN EF-2. THE PATH LENGTH WAS JUST OVER 14 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 200 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT.

NWS STORM SURVEY REPORTED EF1 TORNADO DAMAGE EMBEDDED WITHIN A LARGER AREA OF DOWNBURST DAMAGE ACROSS NORTHEAST CHEROKEE COUNTY. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AS THE TORNADO TRACKED FROM SOUTHEAST OF BLACKSBURG TO YORK COUNTY IN THE AREA AROUND KINGS MOUNTAIN NATIONAL MILITARY PARK.
MULTIPLE TREES AND SOME POWER LINES DOWN ALONG JUMPING BRANCH ROAD AND RAMBLER LANE. LARGE TREE UPROOTED ONTO POWER LINES NEAR MCENTIRE AND BRIDGES. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY REPORTED AN EF1 TORNADO THAT BEGAN IN CHEROKEE COUNTY PASSED THROUGH KINGS MOUNTAIN NATIONAL MILITARY PARK IN YORK COUNTY. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST/NORTHEAST, EITHER ENDING OR LIFTING INTO NORTH CAROLINA IN THE AREA AROUND HENRY KNOB RD AND GRANDVIEW RD N. DAMAGE WAS PRIMARILY LIMITED TO MANY DOWNED TREES.

NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND EF0 TORNADO DAMAGE THAT WAS EITHER A CONTINUATION OF THE TORNADO THAT MOVED ACROSS CHEROKEE AND YORK COUNTIES IN SOUTH CAROLINA, OR WAS A SEPARATE TORNADO. DAMAGE BEGAN AROUND SPARROW SPRINGS RD AND CONTINUED EAST/NORTHEAST BEFORE ENDING NEAR THE GASTONIA MUNICIPAL AIRPORT. DAMAGE WAS PRIMARILY LIMITED TO NUMEROUS UPROOTED TREES, SNAPPED LIMBS, AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED LOGAN BRANCH OUT OF ITS BANKS BEGINNING TO FLOOD SULPHUR SPRINGS RD BETWEEN PLEASANT DR AND HAMRICK RD. TWO TO THREE INCHES OF RAIN FELL WITHIN A 3-HR PERIOD WITH STORM-TOTAL RAINFALL OF 3-4 INCHES.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED BETTS BRANCH OUT OF ITS BANKS CAUSING FLOODING OF ADJACENT FARMLAND AND RESIDENTIAL YARDS. PROVIDED VIDEO SUGGESTS INUNDATION OF AT LEAST 2-4 FEET IN THE LOWEST ELEVATIONS WITH WATER APPROACHING SIGMON DAIRY RD. TWO TO THREE INCHES OF RAIN FELL WITHIN A 3-HR PERIOD WITH STORM-TOTAL RAINFALL OF 3-4 INCHES.

FIRE DEPT/RESCUE REPORTED TANYARD CREEK OUT OF ITS BANKS FLOODING REEPSVILLE RD. FLOODING OF REEPSVILLE RD BEGAN AT APPROXIMATELY 10AM AND PERSISTED UNTIL APPROXIMATELY 1PM. THE CREEK ALSO CAUSED FLOODING OF ADJACENT LOW-LYING AREAS INCLUDING FARMLAND AND RESIDENTIAL YARDS. AT LEAST ONE DRIVEWAY EXPERIENCED SHALLOW FLOODING. TWO TO THREE INCHES OF RAIN FELL WITHIN A 3-HR PERIOD WITH STORM-TOTAL RAINFALL OF 3-4 INCHES OBSERVED.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED BUFFALO CREEK IS OUT OF ITS BANKS AND FLOODING CLEVELAND AVE. TWO TO THREE INCHES OF RAIN FELL WITHIN A 3-HR PERIOD WITH STORM-TOTAL RAINFALL OF 3-4 INCHES.

HEAVY RAINFALL CREATED A LANDSLIDE 30 FEET WIDE AND 2 FEET DEEP. THE LANDSLIDE HAS COVERED AND CLOSED KIRBY MOUNTAIN ROAD NEAR HIGHWAY 321. SEVERAL OTHER ROADS ACROSS THE COUNTY HAVE ALSO BEEN CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
